title: paeonia peregrina var. cretica
intro: Paeonia peregrina var. cretica, also known as the Cretan peony, is a species of flowering plant in the family Paeoniaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the presence of glycosides and other compounds. The health benefits associated with Paeonia peregrina var. cretica include its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, improve digestive health, and exhibit antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and rhizomes are typically used, often in the form of infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
